title: Girlfriends and Boyfriends

jokes.text: I don't kiss and tell.
jokes.setup: Daniel asks if Nick and Lindsay are going out
jokes.punchline: Nick claims discretion while literally just kissed Lindsay in public view
jokes.explanation: Classic teen irony - claiming privacy while being completely obvious. Good character moment showing Nick's awkward attempt at being cool.

jokes.text: Grab me a piece! / Shut up, man. Get outta here.
jokes.setup: Nick trying to have a romantic moment with Lindsay
jokes.punchline: His burnout friend crudely interrupts asking for sexual details
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the social dynamics - Nick's embarrassment shows his genuine feelings while highlighting the crude peer pressure.

jokes.text: I heard he dropped his stick soloing on Black Dog. You know, like that first gig.
jokes.setup: Nick hearing about a drummer who got the gig he wanted
jokes.punchline: Immediately finding fault with the drummer's performance to make himself feel better
jokes.explanation: Great character writing - shows Nick's insecurity and tendency to deflect rejection through criticism. Very human and relatable.

jokes.text: I'm not jealous. I'm really not. I'm glad I bombed the audition, because, you know, if I hadn't... then we wouldn't have kissed, hmm?
jokes.setup: Nick clearly being jealous about losing the drummer spot
jokes.punchline: His transparent attempt to spin failure as destiny for romance
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Nick's rationalization is both pathetic and endearing. The nervous laugh sells the awkwardness perfectly.

jokes.text: Yeah, but Tommy goes to church! Nick is a freak!
jokes.setup: Millie comparing Lindsay's church-going boyfriend to Nick
jokes.punchline: The stark contrast presented as if it's obvious why this matters
jokes.explanation: Perfect Millie dialogue - her naive black-and-white worldview delivered with complete sincerity makes it both funny and character-revealing.

jokes.text: Freaks only date freak girls. And you're not a freak girl. / How do you know? / 'Cause freaks go all the way.
jokes.setup: Millie explaining social hierarchies to Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact delivery of teenage sexual politics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant writing - Millie's clinical analysis of teen social dynamics is both naive and surprisingly accurate, delivered with perfect innocence.

jokes.text: What about Patty Filker? She's a freak girl. And she has a baby! But I guess that's no big deal either.
jokes.setup: Millie providing evidence for her freak girl theory
jokes.punchline: The dramatic escalation to teen pregnancy as proof, delivered with sarcastic innocence
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and character work - Millie's passive-aggressive sarcasm mixed with genuine concern creates perfect cringe comedy.

jokes.text: HO HO, HEY, MERV GRIFFIN! Nice interview.
jokes.setup: Sam having an awkward conversation with Cindy
jokes.punchline: Neal comparing Sam's stilted conversation to a talk show host
jokes.explanation: Sharp and specific reference that perfectly captures Sam's awkward formality. The Merv Griffin comparison is both accurate and cutting.

jokes.text: I've gotta say it rocks pretty heavily, and that's comin' from a guy who's seen Hendrix live.
jokes.setup: Mr. Rosso trying to connect with Lindsay about music
jokes.punchline: His try-hard attempt to establish rock credibility
jokes.explanation: Classic Mr. Rosso - the desperate attempt to be the 'cool' adult by name-dropping his hippie credentials.

jokes.text: I got it on in a van at Woodstock, I'm not judgin' anybody.
jokes.setup: Mr. Rosso trying to be understanding about teen relationships
jokes.punchline: Wildly inappropriate oversharing about his sexual history
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy - Mr. Rosso's complete inability to maintain appropriate boundaries while trying to be the understanding adult.

jokes.text: Until one night, I was checkin' out this discotheque. The one at the bowling alley on 15 Mile.
jokes.setup: Mr. Rosso starting what seems like a cautionary tale
jokes.punchline: The unglamorous reality of his 'discotheque' being at a bowling alley
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of expectations - the sophisticated 'discotheque' being revealed as a bowling alley club is perfectly pathetic.

jokes.text: One thing led to another and... Now I get sores on my lip once a month. I have herpes.
jokes.setup: Mr. Rosso building up to his cautionary tale
jokes.punchline: The incredibly inappropriate revelation to a student
jokes.explanation: Absolutely shocking and hilarious - the complete breakdown of professional boundaries. Mr. Rosso's casual delivery makes it even more horrifying and funny.

jokes.text: Mr. Rosso's devastating confession: 'I have herpes.'
jokes.setup: Long buildup about meeting a girl and things leading to another
jokes.punchline: Blunt, clinical admission that destroys any remaining dignity
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy. The flat delivery and inappropriate context make this devastatingly funny and uncomfortable. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: IT DEVE YOU DON'T WANT IT!
jokes.setup: Mr. Rosso's herpes confession
jokes.punchline: His earnest but bizarre attempt at a health warning
jokes.explanation: The fumbled delivery and desperate emphasis makes his health PSA even more uncomfortable and funny.

jokes.text: I just blew your mind, didn't I?
jokes.setup: Mr. Rosso after his inappropriate herpes confession
jokes.punchline: His proud assumption that he's provided valuable wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mr. Rosso delusion - his complete misreading of the situation and pride in his 'teaching moment' is painfully funny.

jokes.text: Maybe he takes baths instead. / My mom says that they don't really get you clean. / Yeah, you just sit in your own filth.
jokes.setup: Discussing Gordon's hygiene issues
jokes.punchline: The grossed-out escalation about sitting in filth
jokes.explanation: Standard gross-out humor elevated by the innocent delivery and logical progression of disgust.

jokes.text: We're going to be dissecting kittens. [STUDENTS GASP] Just trying to get your attention.
jokes.setup: Teacher announcing what they'll be dissecting
jokes.punchline: The fake-out with kittens, then admitting it was just for attention
jokes.explanation: Effective misdirection joke - the dark humor of threatening kitten dissection followed by the admission of manipulation.

jokes.text: Your lab partner will be Gordon Crisp. / Bill, your partner will be Cindy Sanders.
jokes.setup: Sam desperately wanting Cindy as his partner
jokes.punchline: Getting stuck with the smelly kid while his friend gets his crush
jokes.explanation: Classic cruel irony - Sam's worst case scenario coming true while Bill gets exactly what Sam wanted.

jokes.text: OH, HOW INTERESTING. Me and Cindy, you and Gordon.
jokes.setup: The unfair lab partner assignments
jokes.punchline: Bill's mock-innocent observation of the cruel irony
jokes.explanation: Bill's dry delivery and fake innocence make this a good deadpan moment - he knows exactly how lucky he got.

jokes.text: Well, there goes Cindy's grade point average.
jokes.setup: Bill being paired with Cindy
jokes.punchline: Sam's bitter assumption that Bill will drag down Cindy's grades
jokes.explanation: Good jealous reaction - Sam's petty assumption reveals his bitterness about the partner assignments.

jokes.text: Excuse me, madam. May I take this seat?
jokes.setup: Gordon approaching to sit by Sam
jokes.punchline: His overly formal, courteous manner despite being the smelly outcast
jokes.explanation: Nice character touch - Gordon's formal politeness contrasts with his social outcast status, making him more sympathetic.

jokes.text: Science waits... FOR NO MAN.
jokes.setup: Teacher trying to motivate students to find partners
jokes.punchline: The dramatic pause before the clichéd motivational phrase
jokes.explanation: Decent teacher humor - the overly dramatic delivery of a mundane motivational cliché.

jokes.text: He's a really great guy. / Yeah. I know.
jokes.setup: Daniel continuing to over-emphasize Nick's greatness
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's flat response showing she's not buying it
jokes.explanation: The awkward continuation and Lindsay's deadpan response highlight the forced nature of Daniel's support.

jokes.text: Nick's a stud... you know? I mean, he may not seem like it, but he is.
jokes.setup: Daniel continuing to praise Nick
jokes.punchline: The backhanded compliment that undermines itself
jokes.explanation: Great backhanded compliment - Daniel's attempt at praise actually reveals his low opinion of Nick's obvious qualities.

jokes.text: RIGHT, BUT I THOUGHT WE WERE GOING TO DO THAT AFTER CLASS, MISS YEATS. / HA HA. Be still, my beating heart.
jokes.setup: Student making inappropriate comment to teacher
jokes.punchline: Teacher's sarcastic shutdown of the advance
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with teacher handling inappropriate student behavior through sarcasm.

jokes.text: Yeah, Cindy smells, too. Like flowers. [LAUGHS]
jokes.setup: Discussing how things smell
jokes.punchline: Bill's dreamy observation about Cindy's scent
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Bill's growing crush through innocent observation.

jokes.text: Her tongue was in my ear the whole time. [LAUGHS] / Shut up! / Now I'm gonna have to get Cindy to come and kiss it and make it all better.
jokes.setup: Neal making crude jokes about Cindy
jokes.punchline: The escalation to asking Cindy to kiss his injury
jokes.explanation: Standard teen crude humor with good escalation and Sam's frustrated reaction.

jokes.text: IT'S NEAL-- / OK, so, Bill, I was thinking...
jokes.setup: Neal trying to correct Cindy on his name
jokes.punchline: Being completely ignored and talked over
jokes.explanation: Good cringe beat showing Neal's invisibility to popular kids - a universal teen experience.

jokes.text: To your house? / MM-HMM. / I think--I think I could squeeze you in.
jokes.setup: Cindy asking Bill to come over for homework
jokes.punchline: Bill's attempt to play it cool like he has other options
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Bill's transparent attempt at nonchalance when getting invited to his crush's house.

jokes.text: Cindy's house. 4:30. Should I wear Hai Karate or Old Spice? / Definitely Old Spice.
jokes.setup: Bill excited about going to Cindy's house
jokes.punchline: The serious cologne consultation for a homework session
jokes.explanation: Perfect teen boy moment - treating a study session like a major date. The specific cologne brands add authenticity.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, like the donkeys really give a care. / Well, how would you like some big fat teacher sitting on your back while you had to run around a gym?
jokes.setup: Daniel dismissing Lindsay's concern about donkey basketball
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's empathetic reversal of the situation
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Daniel's callousness vs Lindsay's empathy, with a funny visual image.

jokes.text: But have you seen the Elephant Man? / HMM? / The movie. / OH, OHH! The... guy with-- the deformed guy.
jokes.setup: Nick suggesting a movie date
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's confused and awkward response about the deformed man
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment - Lindsay's uncomfortable reaction to the unusual date suggestion.

jokes.text: YES, MA'AM-- I MEAN, SIR. [LAUGHTER]
jokes.setup: Student responding to teacher's commands
jokes.punchline: Confusing the gender when addressing the teacher
jokes.explanation: Basic gender confusion joke - gets a laugh but nothing special about the execution.

jokes.text: ROMEO AND JULIET. Come on. Last one in class, first one on welfare. It's your choice.
jokes.setup: Teacher trying to motivate students to get to class
jokes.punchline: The dramatic connection between tardiness and welfare
jokes.explanation: Good teacher motivation line with dark humor - the extreme consequence for being late to class.

jokes.text: OH, WHOA! COOL SHEETS! [IMITATES OBI WAN] 'May the Force be with you.' [FORCED LAUGH]
jokes.setup: Cindy seeing Sam's Star Wars sheets
jokes.punchline: Her forced enthusiasm and bad Obi-Wan impression
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment - Cindy's attempt to connect with Sam's interests through awkward pop culture reference.

jokes.text: It is? / Hey, do you think she likes me? / I don't know. Cheerleaders are hard to read that way.
jokes.setup: Sam asking if Cindy likes him
jokes.punchline: Gordon's matter-of-fact analysis of cheerleader psychology
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan wisdom from Gordon - treating cheerleaders like an exotic species to be studied.

jokes.text: No, I'm sorry. My parents don't let us eat junk food.
jokes.setup: Cindy asking if Sam has snacks
jokes.punchline: Sam's embarrassing admission about his parents' health rules
jokes.explanation: Good embarrassment humor - Sam's family restrictions making him seem uncool in front of his crush.

jokes.text: WHAT?! / Well, I don't really watch TV. Except for the Muppet Show.
jokes.setup: Cindy revealing she doesn't watch TV
jokes.punchline: Sam's shocked reaction to this alien concept
jokes.explanation: Good culture clash - Sam's disbelief at meeting someone who doesn't share his primary interest.

jokes.text: [FART-LIKE SOUND] / This stupid chair always does that.
jokes.setup: Cindy sitting in Sam's chair
jokes.punchline: The embarrassing chair sound and her quick excuse
jokes.explanation: Basic fart humor elevated by the awkward social situation and Cindy's quick excuse-making.

jokes.text: Neal's dating advice: 'Find out her schedule and memorize it. That way, you can bump into her and say hi.'
jokes.setup: Sam asking for advice about Cindy
jokes.punchline: Neal's first suggestion is basically stalking
jokes.explanation: Dark humor disguised as innocent teen dating advice - Neal's suggestions are borderline creepy

jokes.text: Being lab partners is like getting stranded on a desert island. You never know what might happen.
jokes.setup: Sam worried about Bill and Cindy being lab partners
jokes.punchline: Neal's dramatic comparison to survival situations
jokes.explanation: Great metaphor that captures teenage relationship anxiety - the idea that proximity breeds romance.

jokes.text: Hey, man, I heard Kim got an 'A' on her world civ test. / That's great. Huh? / Oh, no, that's right. That was my girlfriend.
jokes.setup: Daniel talking about Kim's success
jokes.punchline: His 'correction' emphasizing the girlfriend status
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the running gag - Daniel's passive-aggressive way of emphasizing Lindsay's relationship status.

jokes.text: I heard Kim punched you in the chest really hard. / Oh, whoops. That was me! OHH! AAH!
jokes.setup: Daniel's third 'mistake' about Kim
jokes.punchline: The physical demonstration with actual punch
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and payoff - the running gag builds to actual physical comedy with Nick getting punched.

jokes.text: She eats carrot sticks, and she likes Miss Piggy. / So what? The Muppets are cool. / Yeah, Swedish Chef is kinda cool, but Miss Piggy's lame.
jokes.setup: Bill listing Cindy's strange qualities
jokes.punchline: The boys' serious Muppet character analysis
jokes.explanation: Good nerdy moment - the serious debate about Muppet character quality as relationship criteria.

jokes.text: She cut the cheese. / OOH, MY GOD! / That's not funny. / I heard it, man. I swear.
jokes.setup: Bill revealing embarrassing information about Cindy
jokes.punchline: Sam's horrified reaction to the revelation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the chair incident with Sam's mortified reaction to his crush's humanity being revealed.

jokes.text: It wasn't a squeak. It was the sound of cheese being cut. [GIGGLES]
jokes.setup: The technical chair debate
jokes.punchline: Bill's definitive analysis with giggling
jokes.explanation: Bill's scientific certainty about the fart sound, complete with juvenile giggling, is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I think you're making all of this up because you want me to stop liking her because you like her now. / WHAT?!
jokes.setup: Sam's theory about Bill's motivations
jokes.punchline: Bill's shocked denial of the obvious truth
jokes.explanation: Good insight moment - Sam correctly identifying Bill's growing interest despite Bill's denials.

jokes.text: You changed your hair. / IT JUST LOOKS REALLY FLAT TODAY.
jokes.setup: Sam using Gordon's advice about complimenting hair
jokes.punchline: The backhanded compliment about it looking flat
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Gordon's advice going horribly wrong - Sam's attempt at a compliment becomes an insult.

jokes.text: Sam's hair compliment backfire: 'You changed your hair.' Cindy: 'It just looks really flat today.'
jokes.setup: Sam tries Neal's advice about complimenting hair
jokes.punchline: His attempt to notice changes becomes accidental insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect execution of dating advice gone wrong. Shows how pickup techniques backfire for awkward teens.

jokes.text: I finally got to see Welcome Back, Kotter. You were right. Mr. Woodman is so funny. / Yeah, I'm tellin' ya, that guy is totally underrated.
jokes.setup: Cindy sharing TV enthusiasm with Bill
jokes.punchline: Bill's serious critical analysis of a minor sitcom character
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing Bill and Cindy connecting over what Sam failed to share - Bill's earnest TV criticism.

jokes.text: Remember when we used to love Leif Garrett and we used to take my Dynamite magazines out and kiss his picture?
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to connect with Millie
jokes.punchline: The embarrassing confession about kissing magazine photos
jokes.explanation: Good nostalgic cringe - the universal teen girl experience of celebrity crushes taken to embarrassing extremes.

jokes.text: And we went to see him at the State Fair and cut all the way to the front row. It turned out to be Jack Albertson from Chico and the Man.
jokes.setup: Lindsay recounting their Leif Garrett concert experience
jokes.punchline: The disappointing reveal of the wrong celebrity entirely
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and character detail - the perfect deflation of teen expectations with specific, believable disappointment.

jokes.text: Once you do, you'll never be the same again. Plus no one would want to marry you. Why should they buy the cow when they can get the milk for free?
jokes.setup: Millie warning Lindsay about sex
jokes.punchline: The old-fashioned cow/milk metaphor delivered earnestly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Millie dialogue - the earnest delivery of outdated wisdom that she truly believes in.

jokes.text: Thank you, Sylvia Plath. Uh...it's... it's a little dark. We want to sell yearbooks, not tell the truth.
jokes.setup: Cindy's dark diary entry for the yearbook
jokes.punchline: The teacher's cynical but accurate assessment of yearbook priorities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line that captures both educational cynicism and the commodification of high school experience. The Sylvia Plath reference is perfect.

jokes.text: 'W-A-T-E.' [LAUGHS]
jokes.setup: Lindsay reading Nick's card
jokes.punchline: The hilariously misspelled romantic message
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nick moment - his romantic gesture undermined by basic spelling inability, showing the character's limitations.

jokes.text: I just got my period. So what? Deal with it.
jokes.setup: Cindy explaining her cranky mood
jokes.punchline: The casual, matter-of-fact overshare to Sam
jokes.explanation: Great uncomfortable moment - Cindy's casual attitude about bodily functions that would mortify most teenage boys.

jokes.text: 1/8 for $25?! Sam, that's highway robbery! I can get a full page in a Sunday paper for a hundred.
jokes.setup: Sam trying to sell his dad a yearbook ad
jokes.punchline: Mr. Weir's business-minded outrage at the pricing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mr. Weir's practical business sense clashing with school fundraising reality.

jokes.text: Some of the greatest salesmen worked in teams. / Yeah, but we'll both finish sooner if we split up, don't you think?
jokes.setup: Mr. Weir giving contradictory sales advice
jokes.punchline: Cindy's polite but firm rejection of staying with Sam
jokes.explanation: Good moment showing Cindy's desire to separate from Sam despite his father's well-meaning interference.

jokes.text: Nobody who's 'just a friend' sends a single rose. I know all about single roses. I sent one to your mother, and I know when and why I sent it.
jokes.setup: Lindsay claiming Nick is just a friend
jokes.punchline: Mr. Weir's worldly wisdom about single rose symbolism
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Mr. Weir drawing on his own romantic experience to see through Lindsay's denial.

jokes.text: Nick? Was he the one that ate all my fruit roll-ups? / Well, you said he could have them. / No, I--I know. I...
jokes.setup: Parents trying to identify Nick
jokes.punchline: The mundane but memorable fruit roll-up incident
jokes.explanation: Good parent moment - remembering boyfriends by their snack consumption rather than meaningful interactions.

jokes.text: Your mother and I are not taking care of some 'wedlock' baby, while you're off gallivanting at college.
jokes.setup: Mr. Weir's concerns about Lindsay's boyfriend
jokes.punchline: The dramatic leap to teen pregnancy and college abandonment
jokes.explanation: Good parental catastrophizing - Mr. Weir's mind immediately jumping to worst-case scenarios.

jokes.text: It was the worst $5.00 I've ever spent. And I wish I could get that $5.00 back.
jokes.setup: Mr. Weir's confession about his first sexual experience
jokes.punchline: Focusing on the financial rather than emotional cost
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Mr. Weir's practical mindset reducing a profound regret to dollars and cents.

jokes.text: What your father is trying to say is... that your virginity is... a gift.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Weir trying to salvage the sex talk
jokes.punchline: The awkward pause and euphemistic phrasing
jokes.explanation: Good parental awkwardness - Mrs. Weir's attempt to provide a more appropriate message after her husband's overshare.

jokes.text: I was gonna say she said she thought you were the nicest guy in the school. / Cindy said that? / Yeah.
jokes.setup: Sam getting angry about Bill's crude jokes
jokes.punchline: The genuine compliment that Bill was actually going to share
jokes.explanation: Nice reversal showing Bill's genuine friendship underneath the teasing - the real compliment is actually sweet.

jokes.text: Sam, Bill's been in love with Melissa Karpinski ever since he stood behind her at the Dairy Queen. She was wearing this halter top. [GROANS] She is one foxy lady.
jokes.setup: Neal explaining Bill's true romantic interest
jokes.punchline: The minimal interaction that sparked Bill's crush and his dreamy response
jokes.explanation: Great character detail - the absurdly minimal basis for Bill's crush perfectly captures teenage boy psychology.

jokes.text: She doesn't cut the cheese. [BOTH LAUGH] / Come on! Everybody cuts the cheese.
jokes.setup: Bill defending Melissa against fart accusations
jokes.punchline: Sam's universal truth about human bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Sam's practical wisdom about universal human nature versus Bill's romantic idealization.

jokes.text: You really think I'll fill out? / Yeah, not like me, though. In 3 years, I'm going to be enormous. / Oh, yeah. Me, too. My dad is 6'3".
jokes.setup: Boys discussing their future physical development
jokes.punchline: Their confident predictions about becoming huge and impressive
jokes.explanation: Good teen boy moment - the hopeful delusions about future physical transformation to solve current problems.

jokes.text: SURELY YOU WILL. / OK. / DON'T CALL ME SHIRLEY. [LAUGHS] / BILLY, HAVE YOU EVER SEEN A GROWN MAN NAKED?
jokes.setup: Boys quoting Airplane! movie
jokes.punchline: The escalation to the inappropriate pilot question
jokes.explanation: Standard movie quote humor - boys bonding over inappropriate comedy references.

jokes.text: I know I smell, Sam. / W-W-WHAT ARE YOU TALKING ABOUT? / I may be smelly, but I'm not stupid.
jokes.setup: Sam's awkward question about deodorant
jokes.punchline: Gordon's matter-of-fact acknowledgment of his social status
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Gordon's dignity and self-awareness despite his social outcast status.

jokes.text: It's a medical condition. It's called tryptophanuria. It's genetic. / WOW! Is there a cure? / Not yet, but research is progressing.
jokes.setup: Gordon explaining his smell problem
jokes.punchline: His scientific, hopeful approach to his medical condition
jokes.explanation: Great character development - Gordon's medical explanation adds dignity and hope to his situation.

jokes.text: Nice people don't care, and it weeds out the jerks. My mom says it's a gift.
jokes.setup: Gordon discussing living with his condition
jokes.punchline: His mother's positive reframing of his social disadvantage
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment - Gordon's mother's wisdom turning his disadvantage into a character test for others.

jokes.text: I'll try not to stink up the joint. [SAM LAUGHS] Just kidding. Yeah, I know.
jokes.setup: Gordon agreeing to join the movie outing
jokes.punchline: His self-deprecating joke followed by acknowledging Sam's politeness
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Gordon's humor about his situation and social awareness.

jokes.text: Sam imitating Cindy: '[Imitates Cindy] See you after school.'
jokes.setup: Cindy just asked Sam to hang out
jokes.punchline: Sam's giddy mimicking of her voice shows his excitement
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Sam's barely contained crush excitement

jokes.text: MMM, I love bacon cheeseburgers. / REALLY? / I kinda expected you to like, you know, healthy food like carrots. / YUCK! If I ever saw another carrot again, it'd be too soon.
jokes.setup: Sam's assumption about Cindy's food preferences
jokes.punchline: Cindy's strong rejection of the healthy food stereotype
jokes.explanation: Good character surprise - Cindy defying expectations and showing she's rebelling against her healthy image.

jokes.text: I have, like, the hugest crush on him. / At last week's game, he asked my friend Joe for my phone number. Do you think that means he likes me?
jokes.setup: Cindy revealing her crush on Todd
jokes.punchline: The oblivious question about obvious romantic interest
jokes.explanation: Good teen obliviousness - Cindy's inability to read obvious romantic signals despite being the object of others' crushes.

jokes.text: It's not like with you. You're so easy to talk to. You're just like my sister.
jokes.setup: Cindy comparing Sam to the guy she likes
jokes.punchline: The devastating 'sister' comparison
jokes.explanation: Devastating friend-zone moment - the classic 'like a sister' that crushes romantic hopes completely.

jokes.text: Nothing about you and me should never be rushed. I made that mistake before. But I'm not going to make it with you.
jokes.setup: Nick's romantic speech about taking things slow
jokes.punchline: The grammatical error undermining his profound statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nick moment - his sincere romantic sentiment undermined by his linguistic limitations.

jokes.text: You want to make out or something? / NO. / All guys want to make out. But I just want to hold you.
jokes.setup: Nick's romantic buildup leading to expected proposition
jokes.punchline: His 'enlightened' distinction between making out and holding
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Nick's attempt to be different from 'other guys' while still being predictably male.

jokes.text: Nick's romantic contradiction: 'All guys want to make out. But I just want to hold you.'
jokes.setup: Lindsay suggests making out
jokes.punchline: Nick claims to be different while immediately contradicting himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Nick's self-delusion about being a sensitive romantic

jokes.text: I don't need another friend. I already have 2. I mean, how many more friends does a guy need? [BOTH LAUGH]
jokes.setup: Sam complaining about being friend-zoned by Cindy
jokes.punchline: His practical analysis of his friendship needs
jokes.explanation: Excellent line - Sam's mathematical approach to friendship quotas is both funny and genuinely sad.

jokes.text: OH...NO! NO, NO, NO, I'M NOT BUSY. Tell me what else happened.
jokes.setup: Sam getting a call from Cindy about her date
jokes.punchline: His eager willingness to hear details about her romantic success with someone else
jokes.explanation: Perfect painful ending - Sam's desperation to maintain connection even when it hurts him.

tmdb_overview: Sam tries out to be the school mascot hoping to impress Cindy. Meanwhile, Daniel, Ken, and Kim suddenly become the basketball team's most rabid fans following a series of run-ins with jocks from the rival school, Lincoln High. Lindsay tries to figure out the best way to break up with Nick.
